mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and boosts profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with declining retail sales indicate tightening financial conditions that choke consumer spending, producing negative equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 45% over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals robust economic expansion without pricing pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenues, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months following positive payroll revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 700 thousand units signals a contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, forecasting negative equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and industrial production indicate a recessionary monetary easing phase that initially triggers risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in FEDFUNDS and INDPRO fail to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance at p < 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth during periods of stable consumer prices confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when CPIAUCSL volatility exceeds historical norms, resulting in a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling period.
